trigger: Solving a freshness/cadence problem and reaching for multiple invalidation paths
rule: Default to ONE invalidation knob — usually the simplest LaunchAgent interval — and stop. 15s is imperceptible; sub-second 'event-driven invalidation' chains (Popen-from-writer + watcher + own LaunchAgent) are over-engineering for an operator dashboard. Add the second knob only if the user actually complains about latency, not preemptively. The ergonomic loss of complexity beats the freshness gain almost every time.
why: On 2026-05-22 the dashboard was stale because its plist ran every 120s. Right fix: change interval to 15s. Wrong fix (which I built first): dedicated LaunchAgent + Popen trigger from executor + Popen trigger from detector, three knobs to keep in sync. Mukul: 'Seems like a complicated solution.' Rolled back to one knob. The rule generalizes: invalidation latency is rarely the user's pain — moving-parts complexity is.
